insight: For Buttondown idempotency: use a local issues/YYYY-MM-DD/.published sentinel file written atomically with the issue artifacts in the same git commit, NOT a Buttondown LIST-based check. Buttondown's LIST paginates after several months of issues and the sentinel can land on page N, returning false and publishing a duplicate email. Local file is zero-cost, version-controlled, and immune to external API pagination.

Claude structured-output curation invariant (E-05): instruct the prompt to return exactly one output item per input item, with keep:boolean flag \u2014 Claude MUST NOT filter itself. The post-parse count check is 'scoredItems.length === rawItemsSentToClaude.length'. Filtering happens downstream. Ambiguity between 'one per input' vs 'only kept items' causes E-05 to either always fire (breaking nominal runs) or never fire (silent thin issues). State this explicitly in the system prompt AND add a Zod post-parse length assertion.
When env vars parse to numbers via Number(), guard against NaN explicitly — Number('abc') = NaN silently disables comparisons like count < min. Use Number.isFinite + throw on invalid.
GitHub Actions workflow-command annotations (::error::, ::warning::) are injection vectors if they contain attacker-controlled CR/LF. Sanitize msg text with .replace(/[\r\n]+/g, ' ') before emitting.

For UTC YYYY-MM-DD date derivation in Node, use new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) — stable across node versions, independent of host TZ. Property test at day-boundary (23:58, 00:00) to catch off-by-one.

When a zod 'closed metadata' requirement says 'no Record<string, unknown>', use z.discriminatedUnion on a literal tag field (e.g. source). Tests should assert unknown discriminator value FAILS, not that extra keys fail (zod strips by default).

Reviewers can confuse Python PyPI versions with npm package versions when CLAUDE.md mixes stack proposals. CLAUDE.md anthropic 0.94.1 refers to PyPI; npm @anthropic-ai/sdk latest is 0.90.0. Always verify cross-ecosystem version claims against the actual registry before bumping.

Returning status:'published' from a stub orchestrator path lets future downstream gates silently false-positive. Use a distinct status (stub_no_publisher) and exit non-zero in CLI until the real publisher lands, so scheduled jobs visibly fail rather than silently succeeding.

Per-collector timeout composites must accept an optional parent AbortSignal and forward it to withTimeout. Without this, a SIGTERM / process-level cancel signal is dropped and in-flight HTTP fetches continue until the timer fires, wasting seconds of shutdown time. The timer-local AbortController alone handles the timeout case but not external cancellation.

Redirect resolvers (O-06) need a visited-set loop check in addition to a hop cap. A hop cap alone lets a self-referential 301 silently burn the budget and return a non-canonical URL. Add the target URL to a Set before the next hop and break if it's already present.

E3 pre-filter: order matters — freshness → URL-shape → dedup, then S-05 floor evaluated on unique sources of POST-filter items (not collector status). A source whose items all get filtered out must not count toward the floor or the floor becomes meaningless.

URL normalizer for dedup must strip userinfo (username/password), tracking params (utm_*, fbclid, gclid, gbraid, mkt_tok, _hsenc, mc_eid, ref, etc.), default ports, fragments, AND sort remaining query params. Lowercasing host alone is insufficient — credentialed and non-credentialed variants must collapse to one key.

Pre-filter freshness must distinguish stale (publishedAt < cutoff) from invalid_date (Date.parse → NaN). Lumping them together hides bad collector output behind a benign-looking 'freshnessDropped' counter. Use a 3-state verdict and emit a separate stat + log.warn for invalid_date.

E4 Curator design: inject a pluggable CurationClient (not the SDK) so tests can swap deterministic mocks. Count invariant enforcement lives in the curator, not the client — per-chunk id check + global merge check makes Un-05/E-05 failures surface at the pipeline boundary, not inside an SDK call.

E7 integration verification: keep cross-epic contract tests (C1-C8) in tests/integration/contracts.test.ts and the 10 IV scenarios in scenarios.test.ts with one named describe per scenario; when the link-integrity gate needs to fire in orchestrator tests without Buttondown creds, use DRY_RUN=1 to bypass only the API-key fail-fast (the Un-01 gate still runs before the POST in DRY_RUN).

E-06 backfill: feed the same shared Publisher (built once at orchestrator entry) into BOTH the backfill call AND today's publish — single fail-fast site, single retry config, single secret read. Don't let backfill build its own publisher or it drifts out of sync with prod.

For newsletter-style markdown, expose per-source metadata in a dedicated 'sourceBadge' helper keyed off the discriminated metadata union (HN points, GH stars today+lang, Reddit subreddit+upvotes). Keeps renderer pure, lets the same badge flow into both daily + weekly digests, and survives changes to metadata shape via the zod discriminator.

Vitest's expect(x).toMatchFileSnapshot(path) writes a real .md/.txt file that reviewers can eyeball in a PR — better UX than .snap files for subscriber-facing output like rendered newsletter issues. Pair with '-- -u' to regenerate after intentional renderer changes.

When introducing a central log redactor, require a minimum secret length (e.g., 6 chars) to avoid false-positive substring replacement corrupting legitimate log content. Registering the empty string or short tokens like 'abc' turns every unrelated occurrence into [REDACTED].

CLAUDE.md's 'anthropic SDK 0.94.1' refers to the Python anthropic package on PyPI, not the npm @anthropic-ai/sdk. The npm package's latest is 0.90.0. When an epic says 'bump SDK to X' cross-check both registries — the Python and npm Anthropic SDKs have independent version streams.

Extend existing custom errors with a shared OrchestratorStageError base class early in a project's life — stage-scoped catchers (instanceof base) replace N fragile pattern-matches against concrete classes, and the {stage, cause, retryable} shape gives downstream logging/alerting a stable contract without forcing every new error type to re-invent it.

When removing 'as unknown' casts around raw parsed JSON/XML, extract a typed narrower helper like asRecord(v: unknown): Record<string, unknown> | undefined rather than sprinkling type guards at every callsite. Keeps the cast contained (one authorized boundary) while giving the rest of the code structural safety.

In GitHub Actions, scope API-key secrets to the specific step that needs them via step-level env:, not job-level env:. Job-level env is inherited by every step — so a compromised dependency in pnpm install/test can read publish secrets via process.env. Defence-in-depth even if gitleaks + the orchestrator's registerSecretsFromEnv redaction already provide other layers.

DeepAgents migration trust-contract preservation: when replacing a direct LLM SDK call with an agent framework (LangGraph/DeepAgents/etc.), the C4 link-integrity predicate that sat in the direct-call path MUST remain authoritative over the agent's final output — tool outputs, tool 'validation' results, and agent reasoning must NEVER extend the allowlisted URL set. Enforce via shared URL normalizer (imported from pre-filter layer) with an automated parity test on ≥10 canonicalization variants (mixed case, trailing slash, UTM, percent-encoding, query-order). Separate 'tool input allowlist' (pre-call guard) from 'tool output scrub' (post-call strip) from 'final C4 gate' (post-agent).

When migrating to LangChain/LangGraph-based curation from direct Anthropic SDK, Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control + anthropic-beta header) can be silently stripped by the provider adapter. Cost impact is 2-4× token burn on stable system prompts with no error surfaced. Requires an automated assertion test observing cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on the 2nd chunk of a multi-chunk run. If the adapter strips caching, fallback is to drop to the direct SDK for the model call while keeping LangGraph for state-machine orchestration only.

Vitest's SSR transform does not polyfill import.meta.resolve (it becomes __vite_ssr_import_meta__.resolve which is undefined). When writing ESM code that must resolve package paths AND be testable under Vitest, fall back to createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve() and normalize to a file:// URL. Pattern: check typeof import.meta.resolve first, else use createRequire fallback.

When scaffolding a stub implementation behind an env flag, do NOT make it the default behavior of an existing route. Keep the previous working path as the default and make the new stub explicitly opt-in via an additional flag (e.g. CURATOR_BACKEND=deepagents), so misconfigurations don't silently break users mid-run.

DeepAgents createDeepAgent() unconditionally registers write_todos + filesystem middleware with no opt-out — use createAgent from langchain directly when spec requires DA-U-08-style disable. deepagents stays in closure (version-guarded) for future subagent primitives.

DeepAgent adapter: providerStrategy (from langchain) validates its own JSON-schema translation; when a fakeModel/real-model response violates the schema (e.g. relevanceScore>1), providerStrategy omits the structuredResponse key entirely rather than passing invalid data through. Therefore an explicit 'structuredResponse in result' guard in the adapter fires BEFORE the downstream Zod re-validation, and tests that try to exercise the Zod-fail path through bound violations actually land on the missing-key path.

@langchain/anthropic's usage_metadata.input_tokens is the SUM of real input + cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens (per buildUsageMetadata in message_outputs.js). To compute Anthropic cost comparable to the direct-SDK path (where messages.parse returns them separately), subtract cache_read + cache_creation from the reported input_tokens before multiplying by input rate. Otherwise cache reads get billed at the uncached input rate and cost estimates 2-3× higher than reality.

LangChain's anthropicPromptCachingMiddleware default minMessagesToCache=3 silently disables caching for single-message runs (the zero-tool classifier path sends exactly one HumanMessage plus a systemPrompt — 2 total, below threshold). For classifier-style agents with tools: [], pass minMessagesToCache: 1 explicitly or the DA-U-09 cache assertion will never see cache_read_input_tokens > 0 even though everything else is wired correctly.

When registering LangChain tools via `tool()` under `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`, the zod schema's `_def.description` (`string | undefined` explicit-union) does not unify with the `ZodV3Like` bound's implicit-optional `description?: string`. Fix: cast the schema `as unknown as InteropZodObject` at the call site — same family as the `providerStrategy` cast. Document inline and bump `scripts/check-unchecked-casts.mjs` BASELINE.

DeepAgents tool security: when a tool that probes URLs calls fetch(), always use `redirect: "manual"` and re-validate the `Location` header against the input-set allowlist. `redirect: "follow"` (Node 20 default) lets an attacker-controlled host chase-redirect to 169.254.169.254/etc for SSRF even when the initial URL passed the allowlist.

SSRF allowlists built from untrusted input (RSS/Reddit URLs, user-supplied feeds) MUST filter private/loopback/link-local/multicast hosts at build time. The manual-redirect guard only fires on 3xx — if 169.254.169.254 (AWS metadata) lands directly in the allowlist, the *initial* fetch already hits the internal host. Private-IP host check belongs in the allowlist builder, not downstream.

LangSmith gate must scrub all four env vars LangChain checks — LANGSMITH_TRACING, LANGSMITH_TRACING_V2, LANGCHAIN_TRACING, LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2 (OR'd in @langchain/core/utils/callbacks isTracingEnabled). Scrubbing only 2 leaves bypass paths. Single opt-in flags should also activate the capability, not just remove the force-off — otherwise the flag becomes silent misconfiguration requiring a second operator action.

@anthropic-ai/sdk@0.90 zodOutputFormat helper imports zod/v4 and calls z.toJSONSchema on the caller's schema — it throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading def)' when passed a Zod v3 schema. Workaround: hand-author the JSON Schema and provide a parse callback (see src/curator/curationOutputFormat.ts). The SDK only requires an object with { type: 'json_schema', schema, parse? } in output_config.format.

Reddit .json listings contain entries with invalid URL fields (relative /r/..., whitespace, protocol-less). Do NOT use z.string().url() on the raw Reddit post schema — it throws at listing parse time and kills the entire subreddit batch. Accept z.string(), safeParse each child individually, and normalize URLs in the mapper (relative->https://www.reddit.com{url}, empty->permalink fallback, no-proto->prepend https://).

Claude structured-output calls with system-prompt cache_control can hallucinate ids from PRIOR chunks within the same run (observed 2026-04-19 on chunk 9, reproducibly returning hn-47821814 on all 3 retries). When an id is in the wrong set, don't naive-retry — apply mitigation on attempt N+1: shuffle the payload, drop cache_control, and append a trailing user-turn listing the valid id set. Circuit-break on two consecutive identical unexpected ids to fail fast with a distinct error class instead of burning the retry budget. See src/curator/claudeCurator.ts callWithRetry + ai-builder-pulse-gwv.

Buttondown API requires X-Buttondown-Live-Dangerously: true header on every status: about_to_send POST until a freshly-issued key has been 'dangerously' used once. A prior status: draft POST with the header does NOT flip the flag. Always include the header on every POST regardless of status; it is idempotent and acknowledges that sending is irreversible. Without it, fresh keys return 400 sending_requires_confirmation on first send. Tests must assert header presence on every call (including retries) to guard against silent removal in future refactors.

When using vi.spyOn(fsAtomic, 'writeFileAtomic') to mock a module-namespace import, the consuming module MUST call it as fsAtomic.writeFileAtomic at the call site (not 'const { writeFileAtomic } = fsAtomic' at the top). Destructuring captures the pre-spy reference and the mock never takes effect. Pair with: if the mock chains to real impl, capture 'realWrite = fsAtomic.writeFileAtomic' BEFORE vi.spyOn(...), otherwise realWrite becomes the spy and you self-recurse.

Curated-content sites must validate the protocol of any URL they interpolate into an anchor href, even when the data comes from a trusted pipeline. A hostile or miscurated item with url='javascript:alert(1)' becomes an executable link the moment a visitor clicks. Guard with 'new URL(u, base)' + check protocol is http: or https:, fall back to '#' otherwise. This caught a P1 in the brochure's top-pick rendering — items.json is produced by our own curator but an adversarial source could still land payloads in it.

For static sites that need to reflect 'the latest X' from a repo without redeploys, write a small pointer file (e.g. issues/latest.json with { date, path }) from the writer side rather than enumerating dated folders via the GitHub Contents API at runtime. The API has a 60 req/hr anonymous rate limit per IP; the pointer is O(1), deterministic, CDN-cacheable, and makes the data contract explicit. Cost is one extra writeFileAtomic call in the pipeline.

When flipping a domain-wide URL constant (e.g., buttondown.com/ai-builder-pulse → pulse.ryanhenderson.dev), grep the ENTIRE repo for the old literal string before closing the task, not just the single module being refactored. Constants re-surface in: (a) outgoing email bodies across multiple renderers (daily + weekly digest), (b) site HTML templates, (c) client JS, (d) test fixtures under fixtures/, (e) issue markdown archives. Pattern: add 'git grep <old-literal>' as the last step of any URL/domain migration. Discovered in cook-it epic ai-builder-pulse-d2c where T6 (daily renderer) missed src/weekly/digest.ts:236 and fixtures/rendered/sample-weekly.md; implementation reviewer flagged it as P1.

AC wording should match the EARS verb, not the test location. AC-11 in epic ai-builder-pulse-d2c said 'renderer unit test update' as the verification method, so the agent implementing T6 (daily renderer) didn't realize the weekly digest was also in scope — it's a separate module but also emits 'outgoing email bodies'. Rule: if the EARS requirement says 'the system shall link X to Y', the AC row must say 'no remaining X links across all outgoing surfaces' or enumerate every affected module. Verification method should describe WHAT not WHERE.

For static brochure sites deployed via GH Pages (pages.yml), preview data fetched by client-side JS should be INLINED at build time with the same data, keeping the client fetch as progressive enhancement for cache refresh. This eliminates first-paint skeletons + 404 surprises on relative-path fetches when the server layout differs from the repo layout (issues/latest.json is at repo root under issues/ but the browser expects it at /latest.json because pages.yml hoists it). Replace 'build step that just copies files' with 'build step that assembles AND pre-renders first-paint state'. Discovered in epic ai-builder-pulse-d2c.

verifyLinkIntegrity in this repo (src/curator/linkIntegrity.ts, called from src/orchestrator.ts:521) runs PRE-RENDER on ScoredItems — it inspects item.url and URLs embedded in item.description, NOT the rendered markdown body. URLs added by the renderer/weekly digest at render time (derived fields, template URLs not otherwise covered) therefore do NOT require entries in RENDERER_TEMPLATE_URL_PATTERNS for the Un-01 invariant. Confirmed during epic ai-builder-pulse-acr (HN thread link suffix). Action: when adding render-time URLs, verify this boundary before assuming an allowlist update is needed.

When interpolating a collector-provided string field into rendered markdown, the renderer cannot trust the collector's schema is tight enough. Example: HN Algolia objectID is typed z.string() with no char restriction; a raw interpolation into ([HN](...?id=${id})) would let a ) break out of the link destination. Defense pattern already used in this repo: tighten the regex at the render site so only safe chars produce the output (/^hn-([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)$/), and let malformed ids fall through to the spec's defensive fallback path. Prefer this over mid-string escape because it also doubles as the R5/AC-5 invariant enforcer.

Two-stage curation cost reduction: insert a binary keep/drop Haiku pre-filter stage between the heuristic pre-filter and the expensive Sonnet curator. Cuts items reaching Sonnet by ~40%. CRITICAL design rules that proved correct: (1) every Haiku failure path falls back to pass-through, NOT failure — chunk-level errors keep that chunk's items, stage-level errors set skipped=true and pass everything through; (2) Haiku cost MUST NOT count against the Sonnet CURATOR_MAX_USD ceiling (Sonnet's chunk count would be wrong otherwise); (3) the curator's E-05 count invariant must operate on the post-Haiku item count (curatorInputItems.length), not the heuristic-pre-filter count, or chunked Haiku drops trip a false invariant violation; (4) S-05 source floor check stays BEFORE Haiku — Haiku output is allowed to be source-imbalanced.

When routing the Anthropic SDK through OpenRouter (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api), every distinct model ID needs its own MODEL_OVERRIDE repo variable in the OpenRouter format (anthropic/claude-X-Y.Z with a dot in version, not dash). Default Anthropic-native IDs (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) will get a 400 from OpenRouter. Pattern: pin the Anthropic-native ID in code as MODEL_PIN, accept MODEL_OVERRIDE env var, and document that OpenRouter routing requires setting it.

Haiku pre-filter live results 2026-04-25: 868 items into 9 chunks of 100, 35% drop rate, Haiku cost $0.1052 (69k in + 12k out tokens, anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 via OpenRouter). Sonnet then processed 565 items in 12 chunks for $0.7555. Total: $0.86 vs $1.15 baseline = 25% net savings (34% on Sonnet alone). The 35% Haiku drop rate matches Sonnet's input-token reduction (109k → 70k = -37%), validating the contract: Haiku's keep/drop decisions translate cleanly into reduced Sonnet workload. Conservative threshold from spec proved well-calibrated — no quality regression visible at first run.

When implementing a filter from a spec that references RawItem.metadata fields, verify each field name against the actual zod schema in src/types.ts before coding. Spec text often uses conceptual names (e.g. Reddit 'score') while the schema uses different keys ('upvotes'). The collector source-of-truth (e.g. src/collectors/reddit.ts:231 'upvotes: post.score') resolves the ambiguity. Operator-facing env var names can keep the spec naming (REDDIT_MIN_SCORE) for clarity even when the field is internally 'upvotes'.

When adding a new pre-filter that reads optional RawItem.metadata fields, existing test fixture factories whose metadata defaults to source-only (e.g. {source: 'hn'}) will start failing because the new filter treats undefined as 0. Fix: update the fixture factories (tests/preFilter/applyPreFilter.test.ts, tests/preFilter/hnPatternFilter.test.ts, and ad-hoc literals in tests/orchestrator.test.ts / tests/errorPaths.test.ts) to include sensible engagement defaults (points: 5, numComments: 2) so unrelated tests are not accidentally exercising the new filter. Use sed to bulk-update literal {source: 'hn' as const} occurrences across test files.
